reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails significantly on content accuracy. The required script was "Hahaha, that joke you told was so funny!", but the model generated "Haha, that joke you told was so funny! I couldn't stop laughing. Seriously." The addition of the phrase 'I couldn't stop laughing. Seriously.' is a hallucination not present in the prompt. Regarding the Non-Linguistic Vocalizations (laughter), the 'Haha' at 00:00 sounds more like breathless panting or a forced expulsion of air rather than genuine laughter. The prosody throughout the sentence is somewhat strained and breathless, lacking the natural cadence of a person actually laughing while speaking.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 performs excellently. It correctly interprets the text "Hahaha" as an instruction for Non-Linguistic Vocalization, generating a natural, genuine-sounding laugh from 00:00 to 00:01. It then seamlessly transitions into the speech "That joke you told was so funny," maintaining a 'smiled speech' quality where the laughter serves as a natural underlying tone to the voice. The prosody is fluid and highly human-like, effectively conveying the requested paralinguistic feature.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 hallucinates extra content not requested in the prompt ('I couldn't stop laughing. Seriously'), which is a major failure in content accuracy. Furthermore, Model 1's attempt at laughter sounds unnatural and breathless, resembling panting more than amusement. In contrast, Model 2 generates a high-quality, realistic laugh that matches the 'Hahaha' prompt and integrates the emotion into the subsequent speech naturally. Model 2 follows the instruction perfectly while Model 1 fails on both content and feature quality.
